title: English Thinking
objective: Students will explore english thinking concepts including Author's word choices, Inference vs Explicit Meaning.

steps.content: Let's explore Author's word choices! Have you ever wondered about this?
Recognise how an author's deliberate choices — of words, structure, tone, and perspective — create particular effects on you as a reader
Time to try this out: authorial awareness research
When you reads a book or article, do they ever notice and comment on why the author chose to write it a certain way — and what effect that has on them as a reader?
You did it! Here's what we explored about Author's word choices: authorial awareness research Understanding Author's Purpose (Firkins)
Let's discover the amazing world of Inference vs Explicit Meaning!
Distinguish between what a text explicitly says and what you have inferred, assumed, or read in — knowing which is which is fundamental to honest comprehension
Let's practice together! inference vs literal comprehension development research
After you reads a story or article, can they tell you which parts they actually read in the text and which parts they worked out or assumed for themselves?
You did it! Here's what we explored about Inference vs Explicit Meaning: inference vs literal comprehension development research online inference making and comprehension monitoring (PMC 2021)
